message:
refactor: apply clippy lints and add two-tier progressive search E2E tests

Code quality improvements from clippy and rustfmt across the two-tier
search implementation:

- Replace .map().unwrap_or() with .map_or() for idiomatic Option handling
  (hybrid.rs, storage.rs)
- Replace .or() with .or_else() where the fallback involves a function
  call to avoid eager evaluation (main.rs)
- Use range contains() instead of manual comparison for version check
  (storage.rs)
- Flatten nested if-else into if-else-if chain for clarity in the
  two-tier search blending path (main.rs)
- Hoist shared imports and type aliases above the two-tier branch so they
  are available to both code paths (lib.rs)
- Fix rustfmt line-length issues in test assertions and import groupings
- Add #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)] where justified
- Add panics doc section for load_all_embeddings_filtered
- Fix storage test to assert correct composite PK including model_id

New test coverage:
- Add comprehensive two-tier progressive search E2E tests using hash
  embedder (7 tests covering blending, degradation, boundary factors,
  named index roundtrip, search mode parsing, and config defaults)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
